position: Lead Forward Deployed Engineer / AI Solution Architect
description:
Embedded in the UK Government’s flagship programme to scale AI coding assistants (Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Amazon Kiro, Gemini Code Assist) across 1,000+ engineers in multiple departments. Holding two roles concurrently: leading the FDE community and owning the Adoption & Foundation workstream.

  • Designed the Workstream A operating model from a blank sheet — stakeholder mapping, terms of reference, REACH-based readiness assessment, adoption planning.
  • Built reusable Claude Skills (Adoption Assessment, Adoption Plan, Engagement Retrospective, MaroBot) that ingest call transcripts and produce department-tailored deliverables.
  • Authored a complete .NET legacy modernisation workshop end-to-end with Claude Code; delivered to senior engineers at UKHSA.
  • Bootstrapped the public DSIT AI Engineering Lab repository — quality metrics, incident response, guardrails, manager guides.
  • Pivotal role positioning V1 for the extension (up to £8m, 7 months).
  • Awarded Outstanding quarterly rating Q1 2026 — top ~0.5% of colleagues.

position: AI Technical Lead / Senior AI Engineer
description:
Led delivery of Scribe, a GenAI letter-generation platform, from PoC to production. Scale: 1,500+ advisors, 80,000+ letters/month. Gartner Eye on Innovation runner-up; Digital Leaders ‘AI for Ethics, Trust and Governance’ shortlist.

  • Defined the Cafcass AI capability roadmap; designed the V1 AI Training Plan for Software Engineers.
  • Implemented multi-modal output (text and audio) for neurodiverse children and English-language barriers.
  • Built evaluation frameworks: cosine similarity, ROUGE, Flesch-Kincaid, SMOG.
  • Embedded responsible AI: access controls, audit trails, privacy-by-design, UK data residency. Aligned with ATRS, EU AI Act, Government Service Standard.
  • Pre-sales: Cafcass Scribe (~£350k), Cafcass Document Summarisation (~£350k), Home Office AI Delivery Partner bid (up to £3m).

companyName: Home Office, MoJ & HMCTS — via Version 1
description:
Delivered across multiple high-impact UK Government programmes as a senior engineer at Version 1.

  • Home Office / MAPPS (2022–2024): Authentication and document upload on multi-agency offender management platform projected to save £76m/year.
  • HMCTS Future Hearings & Civil/Citizen (2021–2022): Modernised Small Claims Court portal; implemented Future Hearings platform components.

Developed high-impact platforms and championed engineering best practices at a specialist digital consultancy.

  • Connected over 8,000 employees globally by delivering a knowledge-discovery portal with semantic search, relevance scoring, and Microsoft Teams integration (RAG pipeline using Azure QnA Maker and LUIS).
  • Developed sales management platforms for two clients.
  • Researched and championed Test-Driven Development best practices.
  • Completed penetration and security testing and code reviews.

companyName: SMS Environmental Ltd - Didcot, United Kingdom
description:
Lead developer of a water treatment compliance portal for an environmental services company.

  • Achieved better platform performance through reusable components across the whole project.
  • Implemented a new automation process reducing client-specific delivery time from 6–7 to 2 weeks.

Fully implemented the user interface of an internal portal used by all 39 distribution centres within Smiths News on a daily basis, enabling users to make better decisions in delivery planning through bigger degree of transparency and control over the available information used. Being able to track user actions also increased the level of accountability of employees fulfilling certain roles.


Creating a parcel delivery tracking portal for Pass My Parcel has benefitted stakeholders at all levels. Having a deeper insight into each stage of the delivery process has helped depot supervisors in stock & delivery planning, empowered the support team, and increased customer satisfaction levels.


Completed a number of changes and improvements, as well as a rebranding exercise on a separate website for handling returns.


Due to my implementations of new functionalities in an externally developed but internally used application, the company has saved around £20,000 on third-party development. Enabling the search engine to work on different components, allowing for repeated pre-orders, and implementing numerous bug fixes for improved UI, were all welcomed developments by app users.


Being in charge of the Google Analytics tracking of the application has resulted in further improvement suggestions.


Maintaining approximately 20 websites of the company, and it's connected entities using Drupal. Carried out numerous changes in look and functionality, and also performed content management tasks when it was necessary.

Completing my first assignment enabled the company to reduce processing time by automatically connecting to a remote web application in order to retrieve data.


The prompt and accurate delivery of the most recently updated client databases, combined with the visualization of results for easier understanding, accelerated the decision making on security needs for Risk Management Professionals at the client’s side.


Designed and created an employee platform, which provides transparency and reduces communication response time. Customizing the portal by user permissions gives control to HR managers. It also introduces a new virtual company culture. Containing user profiles, events & holiday planner, reporting facility, automatic notifications, archiving options, and mapping technology, beyond the practicalities the aim is to develop it into a social network and marketing tool, where current workforce can connect to potential interns as well as former employees.


As a result of my interactive Uniform Order application built in an online Application Form, mobilisation timeframes are reduced by 2 days on average.


Being part of a team of 9 software developers, my contribution to several other ongoing projects through consultations, debugging and testing, such as online Application Form & Screening System, Employee Helpdesk, and Manpower Scheduling Portal, did not just greatly contributed to the outcome of these projects but helped me to grow into a real colleague, a team player.

position: Software Programmer, Junior Consultant, and Web Designer
description:
As a web designer I enjoyed the freedom of creating web sites from scratch using my creativity in order to deliver the required message to the end users, by implementing a well-structured and easy-to-use interface.


As a software programmer I learned and developed a recognised ERP system (HansaWorld) through customisation, and integration of client specific modules.


Hidden bugs are everywhere but I improved software quality by having a critical eye and paying attention to details.


In a highly time-consuming role of a consultant I developed my time management skills to be constantly available for customers in need.


As a result of leading a team of 3 as a primary consultant, higher customer satisfaction has been achieved and better feedbacks were given.

Contributed to the business by bringing on different methods and revolutionised the styling used in web design. I had the opportunity to replace old templates and build user friendly and easy-to-maintain web sites from scratch.


Delivered numerous web pages on my own, and several others in teamwork, always carefully listening to requirements and satisfying client needs.


Gave a more appealing look to the company by introducing new graphical elements, which are still in use today.

Restaurant Zuzori, Restaurant Amoret, Caffe Bar Galerie, Tavern Pizzeria Peline


Spent my summer holidays working in hospitality service since I was 15 in order to support myself.


Working as a waiter/bartender at the most popular restaurants of the famous holiday destination in Europe improved my language skills, and formed my highly customer orientated attitude.
